metadata.title: Mountain Ranges of India
metadata.description: Comprehensive guide to India's mountains, peaks, and treks

the_himalayas.name: The Himalayas
the_himalayas.formation: Collision of Indian and Eurasian tectonic plates

greater_himalayas.name: Greater Himalayas (Himadri)
greater_himalayas.funfact: The Greater Himalayas contain all peaks above 8,000m and some of the world's largest glaciers outside the polar regions.

lesser_himalayas.name: Lesser Himalayas (Himachal)
lesser_himalayas.funfact: British established most hill stations here to escape summer heat. The Lesser Himalayas receive the maximum rainfall due to orographic effect.

shiwalik_hills.name: Shiwalik Hills (Outer Himalayas)
shiwalik_hills.funfact: The Shiwaliks are the youngest Himalayan range and are still growing! They're prone to landslides due to soft rock composition.

trans_himalayas.name: Trans-Himalayas
trans_himalayas.funfact: This is where the highest motorable road in the world (Umling La at 5,883m) is located!

famous_mountains.name: Kangchenjunga
famous_mountains.funfact: Mountaineers traditionally stop a few feet below the summit to respect Sikkimese beliefs that the peak is sacred. The mountain is believed to be the abode of deity Dzönga.

famous_mountains.name: Nanda Devi
famous_mountains.national_park: Nanda Devi National Park (UNESCO World Heritage Site)
famous_mountains.restrictions: Climbing banned since 1983 to protect ecosystem
famous_mountains.funfact: The inner sanctuary of Nanda Devi was considered impenetrable until 1934. The Nanda Devi Sanctuary is the most spectacular mountain basin in the world with a ring of peaks above 6,000m.

famous_mountains.name: Kamet
famous_mountains.funfact: Kamet was the highest peak climbed until Annapurna was summited in 1950. It was the first 7,500m+ peak to be climbed.

famous_mountains.name: Saltoro Kangri
famous_mountains.funfact: Located in the Siachen conflict zone. One of the most dangerous peaks due to both mountaineering challenges and military presence.

famous_mountains.name: Saser Kangri I
famous_mountains.funfact: One of the highest peaks in the Karakoram range in India. Extremely remote and rarely climbed.

famous_mountains.name: Mamostong Kangri
famous_mountains.funfact: Its name means 'The Great Black Devil' due to its dark, forbidding appearance.

famous_mountains.name: Nanda Kot
famous_mountains.funfact: Guards the entrance to the Nanda Devi Sanctuary. Called the 'Fortress' because of its formidable appearance.

famous_mountains.name: Trisul
famous_mountains.funfact: The first peak above 7,000 meters ever climbed! Tom Longstaff's ascent in 1907 was a remarkable achievement for its time.

famous_mountains.name: Dunagiri
famous_mountains.funfact: Legend says this is where Hanuman found the Sanjeevani herb to save Lakshmana in the Ramayana.

famous_mountains.name: Bandarpunch
famous_mountains.funfact: According to legend, Hanuman's tail caught fire here while bringing the Sanjeevani herb. The monkey-tail shaped ridge gives it the name.

famous_mountains.name: Kedarnath Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Overlooks the sacred Kedarnath Temple, one of the Char Dhams. Not to be confused with the temple at 3,583m.

famous_mountains.name: Shivling
famous_mountains.funfact: Considered one of the most beautiful peaks in the Himalayas. Its distinctive Matterhorn-like shape makes it iconic. Called the 'Matterhorn of India'.

famous_mountains.name: Meru Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: The Shark's Fin route on Meru is considered one of the most difficult climbs in the world. Featured in the documentary 'Meru' (2015).

famous_mountains.name: Stok Kangri
famous_mountains.funfact: One of the most popular trekking peaks in India. Non-technical climb makes it accessible to beginners, yet it's over 6,000m!

famous_mountains.name: Nun
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in Ladakh. The Nun-Kun massif has two peaks - Nun and Kun - visible from Kargil.

famous_mountains.name: Kun
famous_mountains.funfact: Sister peak to Nun. The two peaks are connected by a snowy plateau.

famous_mountains.name: Leo Pargial
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in Himachal Pradesh. Located near the Indo-Tibetan border.

famous_mountains.name: Mount Kailash
famous_mountains.funfact: No one has ever climbed Mount Kailash out of respect for its sacred status. It's the only major peak that remains unclimbed by choice, not difficulty. Believed to be the abode of Lord Shiva.

famous_mountains.name: Kabru
famous_mountains.funfact: Part of the Kangchenjunga massif. Offers stunning views of Kangchenjunga.

famous_mountains.name: Siniolchu
famous_mountains.funfact: Called the 'Most Beautiful Peak in the World' by Douglas Freshfield. Its perfect pyramid shape is iconic.

famous_mountains.name: Pauhunri
famous_mountains.funfact: One of the highest peaks on the Sikkim-Tibet border. Sacred mountain in Sikkim.

famous_mountains.name: Gorichen
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in Arunachal Pradesh. Sacred to the Monpa people. One of the least explored major peaks in India.

famous_mountains.name: Kangto
famous_mountains.funfact: Second highest peak in Arunachal Pradesh. Located on the India-Tibet border.

famous_mountains.name: Nyegi Kangsang
famous_mountains.funfact: One of the highest peaks in eastern Himalayas. Rarely climbed due to remote location.

famous_treks.name: Chadar Trek (Frozen River Trek)
famous_treks.funfact: You literally walk on a frozen river! The Zanskar River freezes in winter, creating the only road into the Zanskar valley. This trek is disappearing due to climate change - the river doesn't freeze as solidly anymore.

famous_treks.name: Valley of Flowers Trek
famous_treks.funfact: British mountaineer Frank Smythe 'discovered' this valley in 1931 after losing his way. Over 600 species of flowering plants bloom here - many found nowhere else on Earth. It was unknown to the outside world until then!

famous_treks.name: Roopkund Trek (Skeleton Lake)
highlights: Views of Trisul and Nanda Ghunti
famous_treks.funfact: The lake contains hundreds of human skeletons from around 850 AD! Scientists believe they were pilgrims killed by a sudden, severe hailstorm. The mystery of these 9th-century skeletons has puzzled researchers for decades.

famous_treks.name: Kedarkantha Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Perfect winter trek! The summit offers panoramic views of over 13 Himalayan peaks including Swargarohini, Bandarpoonch, and Kala Nag. Legend says this is where Lord Shiva meditated.

famous_treks.name: Har Ki Dun Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Legend says the Pandavas (from Mahabharata) passed through this valley on their way to heaven. The Swargarohini peak here is believed to be the 'stairway to heaven' where Yudhishthira ascended.

famous_treks.name: Goechala Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Offers the closest views of Kangchenjunga (8,586m) possible! At sunrise from Goechala, the mountain turns golden, pink, then white in minutes. The trek passes through pristine Kanchenjunga National Park.

famous_treks.name: Sandakphu Trek
highlights: View of 4 of world's 5 highest peaks
famous_treks.funfact: The ONLY place in the world where you can see four of the five highest peaks (Everest, Kangchenjunga, Lhotse, Makalu) in a single view! The ridgeline forms the India-Nepal border.

famous_treks.name: Markha Valley Trek
famous_treks.funfact: The Markha Valley is home to snow leopards! You trek through their habitat. The valley has ancient Buddhist villages that seem frozen in time.

famous_treks.name: Hampta Pass Trek
famous_treks.funfact: You start in green Kullu and end in barren Lahaul - two completely different landscapes in one trek! The pass acts as a climate barrier between lush and desert regions.

famous_treks.name: Pin Parvati Pass Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of India's most challenging treks! Crosses from fertile Parvati Valley to barren Spiti Valley over a technical glacier pass. Snow bridges, crevasses, and high altitude make it extreme.

famous_treks.name: Rupin Pass Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Features some of the most dramatic scenery in Indian Himalayas! The descent after the pass is through a narrow snow gully that's almost vertical in places.

famous_treks.name: Triund Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Most accessible Himalayan trek! You can start from McLeodganj after breakfast and be at the ridge by lunch. Views of Moon Peak and Dhauladhar range are spectacular.

famous_treks.name: Brahmatal Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Legend says Lord Brahma meditated here! The lake freezes completely in winter, and you walk on it. Views of Roopkund peak and Trisul are stunning.

famous_treks.name: Beas Kund Trek
famous_treks.funfact: You trek to the legendary source of the Beas River! Named after sage Vyas who wrote the Mahabharata. The glacial lake feeding the Beas is surrounded by moraines.

famous_treks.name: Kuari Pass Trek (Curzon Trail)
famous_treks.funfact: Named after Lord Curzon (Viceroy of India) who trekked here in 1905! Offers one of the best views of Nanda Devi and over 20 peaks in a single panorama.

famous_treks.name: Dzukou Valley Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Called the 'Valley of Flowers of the Northeast'! Home to the rare Dzukou Lily found nowhere else on Earth. The undulating hills look like waves.

famous_treks.name: Phulara Ridge Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of the few treks where you walk ON the ridge most of the time! Offers 270-degree views throughout. Feels like walking on top of the world.

famous_treks.name: Nag Tibba Trek
highlights: Highest peak in lower Himalayas of Garhwal
famous_treks.funfact: Perfect weekend trek from Delhi! 'Nag Tibba' means 'Serpent's Peak' - there's a temple to the snake god at the top. Offers views of Swargarohini, Bandarpunch, and Gangotri peaks.

famous_treks.name: Tarsar Marsar Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Features two of Kashmir's most beautiful alpine lakes! Tarsar is deep blue, Marsar is green. The meadows around are carpeted with wildflowers in summer.

famous_treks.name: Great Lakes of Kashmir Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Passes through 7 stunning alpine lakes in one trek! Each lake has a different color - emerald, turquoise, deep blue. Considered one of the most beautiful treks in India.

famous_treks.name: Stok Kangri Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of the highest non-technical peaks you can climb! No mountaineering experience needed (though fitness is crucial). Summit views include K2 on clear days!

famous_treks.name: Kanchenjunga Base Camp Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Multi-day expedition into the heart of Kangchenjunga region! One of the most remote and challenging treks in India. Crosses through primeval forests and over glacial moraines.

famous_treks.name: Auden's Col Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of India's most technical treks! Involves crossing Auden's Col, a challenging glacial pass. Only for experienced mountaineers. Named after British geologist John Auden.

famous_treks.name: Panch Kedar Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Pilgrimage to five ancient Shiva temples, each representing a body part of Lord Shiva. Tungnath at 3,680m is the world's highest Shiva temple!

general_funfacts: The Himalayas are still rising by about 1cm per year due to ongoing tectonic collision!
Mount Everest was originally called 'Peak XV' before being named after British surveyor George Everest.
The Himalayas contain the largest deposit of snow and ice outside the polar regions.
The Ganges, Indus, Brahmaputra, and dozens of major rivers originate in the Himalayas.
The Himalayas block cold Central Asian air, creating the monsoon climate of South Asia.
If the Himalayas didn't exist, the Indian subcontinent would be a desert!
The Himalayas have 9 of the world's 10 highest peaks.

karakoram_range.name: Karakoram Range
notable_glaciers: Siachen Glacier (76 km - world's second longest non-polar glacier)

famous_mountains.name: Saser Kangri I
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak of Karakoram range within India. Located in the Saser Muztagh subrange.

famous_mountains.name: Saser Kangri II East
famous_mountains.funfact: Part of the Saser massif. Extremely remote and rarely climbed.

famous_mountains.name: Mamostong Kangri
famous_mountains.funfact: Named for its dark, forbidding appearance. Part of the Rimo Muztagh subrange.

famous_mountains.name: Rimo I
famous_mountains.funfact: Located near the Siachen Glacier. 'Rimo' means striped mountain in local language.

famous_mountains.name: Apsarasas Kangri
famous_mountains.funfact: Apsaras are celestial dancers in Hindu mythology. The peak has multiple summits.

famous_mountains.name: Teram Kangri I
famous_mountains.funfact: Located in the disputed Siachen region. One of India's higher Karakoram peaks.

famous_treks.name: Siachen Base Camp Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Trek to the base of the world's highest battlefield! The Siachen Glacier at 76 km is the longest glacier in the Karakoram. Indian Army has been stationed here since 1984.

famous_treks.name: Saser La Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Ancient trade route connecting Nubra to Central Asia. The pass was used by caravans for centuries.

famous_treks.name: Nubra Valley Trek
famous_treks.funfact: You'll see Bactrian camels in the desert at 10,000 feet! The camels are descendants of Silk Road trading animals. Hunder village has bizarre cold desert sand dunes.

karakoram_range.funfact: The Karakoram has more glaciers and permanent snow than any mountain range outside the polar regions! The Siachen Glacier alone is 76 km long - you could fit the entire Manhattan Island on it multiple times.

ladakh_range.name: Ladakh Range

famous_mountains.name: Stok Kangri
famous_mountains.funfact: Most popular trekking peak in India! Non-technical climb suitable for beginners. You can summit a 6,000m peak without mountaineering experience.

famous_mountains.name: Kang Yatze I
famous_mountains.funfact: Challenging climb in the Markha Valley. Two peaks - Kang Yatze I (technical) and II (trekking peak).

famous_mountains.name: Mentok Kangri
famous_mountains.funfact: Located near the beautiful Tso Moriri Lake. Popular expedition peak.

famous_treks.name: Markha Valley Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Passes through snow leopard habitat! The traditional villages along the route seem frozen in time. Kongmaru La offers views of Stok Kangri and beyond.

famous_treks.name: Stok Kangri Expedition
famous_treks.funfact: One of the easiest 6,000m+ peaks to climb! Non-technical route but very demanding due to altitude. Summit day starts at 1 AM!

famous_treks.name: Sham Valley Trek (Baby Trek)
famous_treks.funfact: Called 'Baby Trek' because it's the easiest in Ladakh! Perfect for acclimatization. Passes through ancient Buddhist villages with 500-year-old monasteries.

famous_treks.name: Lamayuru to Alchi Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Connects two of Ladakh's most ancient monasteries! Alchi has 1,000-year-old Buddhist murals that are UNESCO-worthy. Lamayuru's 'moonland' looks like lunar landscape.

famous_treks.name: Rumtse to Tso Moriri Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Trek through the habitat of Tibetan wild ass (kiang)! The Changpa nomads you meet still live as their ancestors did 1,000 years ago. Tso Moriri is one of the highest lakes in the world.

ladakh_range.funfact: The Ladakh Range is so high and dry that it receives less rainfall than the Sahara Desert! Yet it's surrounded by the highest mountains on Earth. The rain shadow effect creates this 'cold desert' environment.

zanskar_range.name: Zanskar Range

famous_mountains.name: Nun
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in the Zanskar Range. Part of the famous Nun-Kun massif visible from Kargil.

famous_mountains.name: Kun
famous_mountains.funfact: Sister peak to Nun. The two peaks are connected by a high snowy plateau. More accessible than Nun.

famous_mountains.name: Z1 (Karsha Ri)
famous_mountains.funfact: Popular expedition peak in Zanskar. Named 'Z1' as the highest peak in the Zanskar region.

famous_treks.name: Chadar Trek
famous_treks.funfact: You literally walk on a sheet of ice ('Chadar') on the frozen Zanskar River! The only way into Zanskar in winter. Climate change is making this trek increasingly rare - the river doesn't freeze solidly anymore.

famous_treks.name: Zanskar Valley Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Phuktal Monastery is built into a cliff face and is one of the most spectacular monasteries in the Himalayas! Zanskar was completely cut off from the world until the 1980s.

famous_treks.name: Padum to Darcha Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Ancient trade route connecting Zanskar to Lahaul! Crosses the mighty Shingo La. You literally walk from Ladakh to Himachal Pradesh.

zanskar_range.funfact: Zanskar is so isolated that until the 1980s, there were no roads! The only access was by trekking over high passes or walking the frozen river in winter. Some villages still have no road access.

pir_panjal_range.name: Pir Panjal Range

famous_passes.name: Pir Panjal Pass
famous_passes.funfact: The range takes its name from this pass. 'Pir' means saint - named after a Muslim saint.

famous_passes.name: Rohtang Pass
famous_passes.funfact: Name means 'Pile of Corpses'! Many travelers died crossing it in ancient times. Now bypassed by Atal Tunnel.

famous_passes.name: Banihal Pass
famous_passes.funfact: Main route into Kashmir from Jammu. Now bypassed by the Jawahar Tunnel.

famous_mountains.name: Pir Panjal Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: The range is named after this peak and the saint who meditated here.

famous_mountains.name: Tatakoti Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Popular trekking destination near Srinagar.

famous_treks.name: Great Lakes of Kashmir Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Seven stunning alpine lakes in one trek! Each lake is a different shade of blue or green. Vishansar and Krishansar are named after the Hindu god Vishnu and Krishna.

famous_treks.name: Tarsar Marsar Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Two stunning lakes - Tarsar (blue) and Marsar (green). The meadows around are carpeted with wildflowers in summer. One of the most beautiful treks in India.

famous_treks.name: Nafran Valley Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Less crowded alternative to other Kashmir treks. Features pristine meadows and pine forests.

pir_panjal_range.funfact: The Pir Panjal receives the heaviest snowfall in India! Some areas get over 10 meters of snow annually. The range acts as a barrier, trapping moisture from the plains.

dhauladhar_range.name: Dhauladhar Range

famous_mountains.name: Hanuman Tibba
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in the Dhauladhar Range. Named after Lord Hanuman. Popular mountaineering objective.

famous_mountains.name: Moon Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Iconic peak visible from Dharamshala. Named for its crescent shape. Popular with climbers.

famous_mountains.name: Indera Pass Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Gateway to the Chamba Valley. The pass is at 4,342m and offers stunning views.

famous_mountains.name: Mun Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Challenging technical climb near Dharamshala.

famous_treks.name: Triund Trek
famous_treks.funfact: India's most accessible Himalayan trek! You can start after breakfast and be at the ridge by lunch. The ridge offers a perfect front-row view of the Dhauladhar Range.

famous_treks.name: Indrahar Pass Trek
highlights: Views of Pir Panjal and Dhauladhar
famous_treks.funfact: This pass was used by traders for centuries to cross between Kangra and Chamba valleys. You literally walk from one valley to another across the mighty Dhauladhar!

famous_treks.name: Kareri Lake Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Less crowded alternative to Triund! The glacial lake is surrounded by deodars and reflects the Dhauladhar peaks. Perfect for solitude seekers.

famous_treks.name: Minkiani Pass Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of the most challenging treks in Dhauladhar. Crosses from Kangra to Chamba through pristine wilderness.

dhauladhar_range.funfact: The Dhauladhar Range rises so dramatically from the Kangra Valley that you go from 800m to 5,000m in just 25 km horizontal distance! This is one of the steepest elevation gradients in the Himalayas.

aravalli_range.name: Aravalli Range
aravalli_range.age: 1.8 billion years (oldest mountain range in India!)

famous_mountains.name: Guru Shikhar
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in Rajasthan and the Aravallis! Has a temple dedicated to Guru Dattatreya at the top. The only hill station in Rajasthan is here.

famous_mountains.name: Ser (Sirohi Peak)
famous_mountains.funfact: Second highest peak in the Aravallis. Part of the Mount Abu massif.

famous_mountains.name: Achalgarh
famous_mountains.funfact: Has an ancient Shiva temple dating to the 14th century. The name means 'Immovable Fort'.

famous_mountains.name: Taragarh
famous_mountains.funfact: One of the oldest hill forts in India! Built in the 6th century. Called 'Gibraltar of Rajputana' by the British.

famous_mountains.name: Ranthambore Hills
famous_mountains.funfact: Home to the famous Ranthambore Fort and Tiger Reserve. Tigers actually climb these hills!

famous_treks.name: Mount Abu Circuit Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Trek through the only hill station in Rajasthan! The Dilwara Temples here are considered the most beautiful Jain temples in India, with marble carvings so fine that marble appears like lace.

famous_treks.name: Kumbhalgarh Wall Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Walk along the second longest wall in the world after the Great Wall of China! The 36 km wall is wide enough for 8 horses to ride abreast. Built by Rana Kumbha in the 15th century.

famous_treks.name: Jaisamand to Kumbhalgarh Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Walk through the Aravalli wildlife corridor where leopards still roam! Pass through Bhil tribal villages unchanged for centuries.

famous_treks.name: Sariska Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Trek through tiger territory! The nearby Bhangarh Fort is officially listed as 'haunted' by the Archaeological Survey of India - entry is prohibited after sunset!

famous_treks.name: Delhi Ridge Trail
famous_treks.funfact: The Aravallis extend all the way to Delhi! The Delhi Ridge is the northernmost extension and is Delhi's 'green lung'. You can trek through forested trails just 20 minutes from Connaught Place.

aravalli_range.funfact: The Aravallis are 1.8 billion years old - older than the Atlantic Ocean, older than most life on Earth! They were once as tall as the Himalayas but have eroded over billions of years. They stop the Thar Desert from spreading east into the fertile Gangetic plains.

vindhya_range.name: Vindhya Range
vindhya_range.location: Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, Uttar Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Bihar
vindhya_range.separates: North India from South India (geographically and culturally)
significance: Historical barrier between North and South

famous_mountains.name: Amarkantak
famous_mountains.funfact: Sacred source of the Narmada and Son rivers! Called 'King of Pilgrimage Sites' (Teerth Raj). The Narmada begins as a small spring here.

famous_mountains.name: Maikal Hills
famous_mountains.funfact: Contains the Achanakmar Wildlife Sanctuary with significant tiger population.

famous_mountains.name: Kaimur Hills
famous_mountains.funfact: Eastern extension of Vindhyas. Has numerous waterfalls and prehistoric rock paintings.

famous_treks.name: Amarkantak Pilgrimage Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Walk to where the sacred Narmada River is born! The Mai ki Bagiya is a beautiful garden at the exact source. Ancient temples dot the landscape.

famous_treks.name: Pachmarhi Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Called the 'Queen of Satpura'! The Pandava Caves here are believed to be where the Pandavas hid during exile. 10,000-year-old rock paintings exist in caves.

famous_treks.name: Ken-Son Linking Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Walk along the controversial Ken-Son river linking project area. See the landscape that may soon be underwater.

vindhya_range.funfact: The Vindhyas were considered such a significant barrier that ancient texts describe them as the boundary between 'Aryavarta' (North) and 'Dakshinapatha' (South). The legendary sage Agastya is credited with 'crossing' the Vindhyas to spread Vedic culture to South India!

satpura_range.name: Satpura Range
rivers: Forms southern bank of Narmada Valley

famous_mountains.name: Dhupgarh
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest point in Madhya Pradesh! Famous for sunrise and sunset views. Name means 'Place of Sunshine'.

famous_mountains.name: Chauragarh
famous_mountains.funfact: Sacred Shiva temple at the top reached by climbing 1,350 steps! Pilgrims carry heavy tridents up on Mahashivratri.

famous_mountains.name: Mahadeo Hills
famous_mountains.funfact: Sacred hills named after Lord Shiva. Multiple caves and temples.

famous_mountains.name: Rajpipla Hills
famous_mountains.funfact: Western extension of Satpura into Gujarat. Heavily forested tribal region.

famous_treks.name: Pachmarhi Hill Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Trek through Madhya Pradesh's only hill station! The Bhimbetka rock shelters nearby have 30,000-year-old paintings - UNESCO World Heritage Site.

famous_treks.name: Chauragarh Temple Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Climb 1,350 steps to the sacred Shiva shrine! On Mahashivratri, pilgrims carry heavy iron tridents all the way up. Said to be where Shiva rested while carrying Sati's body.

famous_treks.name: Satpura Tiger Reserve Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of the ONLY tiger reserves in India where walking safaris are allowed! You can actually trek through tiger territory on foot. Also has boat and elephant safaris.

famous_treks.name: Toranmal Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Maharashtra's secret hill station! Far less crowded than Mahabaleshwar. The Lotus Lake is covered with lotus flowers in season.

satpura_range.funfact: The Satpura Range runs parallel to the Vindhyas with the Narmada River flowing between them! This creates the famous 'Narmada Rift Valley'. The name 'Satpura' means 'Seven Folds' referring to seven mountain ranges (though geologists count only one continuous range).

western_ghats.name: Western Ghats
western_ghats.age: 150 million years (formed when India separated from Gondwana)
western_ghats.location: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu
western_ghats.endemic_species: 5,000+ flowering plants, 139 mammal species, 508 bird species

famous_mountains.name: Anamudi
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in South India! Home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr. The name refers to its shape resembling an elephant's head. Permits required for trekking.

famous_mountains.name: Doddabetta
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in Tamil Nadu! Has a telescope house at the top. Visible from Ooty town. Covered in shola-grassland ecosystem.

famous_mountains.name: Mullayanagiri
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in Karnataka! Has a small temple at the top. Popular weekend trek from Bengaluru. Covered in grasslands with stunning views.

famous_mountains.name: Kudremukh
famous_mountains.funfact: One of the most beautiful peaks in Western Ghats. Part of Kudremukh National Park. The peak looks like a horse's face when viewed from a distance.

famous_mountains.name: Tadiandamol
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in Coorg district. Sacred to the Kodava people. Popular trek with stunning sunrise views.

famous_mountains.name: Kalsubai
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in Maharashtra! Has a temple of goddess Kalsubai at the top. Popular trek from Mumbai. Iron ladders installed at steep sections.

famous_mountains.name: Harishchandragad
famous_mountains.funfact: Ancient fort with a stunning Konkan Kada cliff. The Kokan Kada is a vertical cliff face dropping 1,000+ feet. Popular and challenging trek.

famous_mountains.name: Rajgad
famous_mountains.funfact: Was the capital of Maratha Empire under Shivaji for 26 years! One of the most important forts in Maharashtra history.

famous_mountains.name: Agasthyamalai
famous_mountains.funfact: Named after sage Agastya who is believed to have meditated here. Southern tip of Western Ghats. Extremely biodiverse.

famous_mountains.name: Meesapulimala
famous_mountains.funfact: Second highest peak in South India! Named because the mountain looks like a leopard with a moustache. Permits required.

famous_mountains.name: Chembra Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Has a heart-shaped lake near the summit! Popular trek in Wayanad. The heart-shaped pond never dries up.

famous_treks.name: Kalsubai Peak Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Popular weekend trek from Mumbai! Iron ladders installed at steep sections make it accessible. The temple at top is dedicated to goddess Kalsubai.

famous_treks.name: Harishchandragad Trek
famous_treks.funfact: The Konkan Kada is a stunning overhang cliff with a 1,000-foot vertical drop! One of Maharashtra's most dramatic treks. The ancient temple cave has a Shiva lingam surrounded by water.

famous_treks.name: Kudremukh Trek
famous_treks.funfact: The peak resembles a horse's face when viewed from certain angles! The grasslands here are incredibly beautiful. Heavy restrictions apply - booking required months in advance.

famous_treks.name: Kumara Parvatha Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of the toughest treks in Karnataka! The climb is incredibly steep. Also called Pushpagiri. Home to rare wildlife and plants.

famous_treks.name: Tadiandamol Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Trek through coffee plantations to the highest peak in Coorg! The Kodava people consider this mountain sacred. Stunning sunrise views.

famous_treks.name: Anamudi Summit Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Summit the highest peak in South India! Special permits required. You may see endangered Nilgiri Tahr along the way. The neelakurinji flower blooms here once every 12 years.

famous_treks.name: Meesapulimala Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Second highest peak in South India! Named because the mountain looks like a moustached leopard. Permits required from Tamil Nadu Forest Department.

famous_treks.name: Chembra Peak Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Features a famous heart-shaped lake that never dries! One of the most popular treks in Kerala. The heart shape is natural and quite photogenic.

famous_treks.name: Rajmachi Fort Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Features two small forts - Shrivardhan and Manaranjan! Spectacular during monsoon with numerous waterfalls. Popular night trek from Mumbai.

famous_treks.name: Rajgad Fort Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Shivaji Maharaj's capital for 26 years! His queen Saibai and mother Jijabai spent time here. The fort is so large it has three separate plateaus (machis). One of the most historically significant forts in India.

famous_treks.name: Sinhagad Fort Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Site of the legendary Battle of Sinhagad where Tanaji Malusare died conquering the fort! Shivaji reportedly said 'Gad aala pan Sinha gela' (We got the fort but lost the Lion). Now a popular weekend getaway from Pune.

famous_treks.name: Lohagad Fort Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Name means 'Iron Fort' due to its impregnability! The distinctive 'Vinchukata' (scorpion tail) fortification makes it unique. Connected to twin fort Visapur by a saddle.

famous_treks.name: Dudhsagar Falls Trek
famous_treks.funfact: The waterfall looks like 'Sea of Milk' (Dudhsagar)! You walk along active railway tracks to reach it. The 310m falls cascade down in four tiers. Made famous by the movie 'Chennai Express'.

famous_treks.name: Kodachadri Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Sacred peak with Mookambika Temple nearby! One of the most picturesque peaks in Karnataka. Dense shola forests and grasslands. The sunset from the peak is legendary.

famous_treks.name: Brahmagiri Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Part of Brahmagiri Wildlife Sanctuary! The peak offers 360-degree views of Coorg's coffee country. The trek passes through diverse ecosystems from coffee to shola forest.

famous_treks.name: Agumbe Rainforest Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Called the 'Cherrapunji of the South' - receives over 7,000mm rainfall annually! This is the King Cobra capital of India. The Agumbe Rainforest Research Station studies these magnificent snakes.

famous_treks.name: Velliangiri Mountains Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Called 'Kailash of the South'! Pilgrims climb seven hills to reach the Shiva temple at the top. Isha Foundation's Sadhguru has made this trek popular. Ancient yogi tradition site.

famous_treks.name: Perumal Peak Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Highest point in the Palani Hills! Sacred to local tribes. Offers views stretching to Kerala on clear days.

famous_treks.name: Nilgiri Tahr Trail (Eravikulam)
famous_treks.funfact: Walk among the endangered Nilgiri Tahr who are remarkably unafraid of humans! The Neelakurinji flower turns entire mountainsides purple-blue every 12 years (next: 2030).

western_ghats.funfact: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas! They formed 150 million years ago when India broke from Gondwana. Despite being heavily eroded, they're one of the world's 8 'hottest' biodiversity hotspots - more species-rich per square kilometer than almost anywhere else on Earth.

eastern_ghats.name: Eastern Ghats
eastern_ghats.location: Odisha, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka (small portion)

famous_mountains.name: Arma Konda
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in the Eastern Ghats and in Andhra Pradesh! Located in the Ananthagiri Hills. Remote and rarely visited.

famous_mountains.name: Jindhagada Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Some surveys list this as the highest in Eastern Ghats. Located in Koraput district near Araku.

famous_mountains.name: Mahendra Giri
famous_mountains.meaning: Named after Lord Parashurama's father Jamadagni (also called Mahendra)
famous_mountains.funfact: Second highest peak in Odisha! Sacred mountain with ancient temples. Legend says Parashurama meditated here after killing the Kshatriyas.

famous_mountains.name: Deomali
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in Odisha! Located near Daringbadi, called the 'Kashmir of Odisha'. Has pine forests unusual for Eastern Ghats.

famous_mountains.name: Shevaroy Hills (Yercaud)
famous_mountains.funfact: Home to the Yercaud hill station! Called 'Poor Man's Ooty'. The Servarayan Temple at the highest point is sacred to local tribes.

famous_mountains.name: Kolli Hills (Kolli Malai)
famous_mountains.funfact: The road to the top has 70 hairpin bends! Called 'Mountains of Death' due to dangerous roads and malaria (now eradicated). Has ancient Arapaleeswarar Temple.

famous_mountains.name: Javadi Hills
famous_mountains.funfact: Largest hill range in Tamil Nadu by area! Home to the Malayali tribal community. Remote and less developed.

famous_mountains.name: Simhachalam
famous_mountains.funfact: Famous for the ancient Varaha Lakshmi Narasimha Temple dating to 11th century. One of the most important Vaishnavite sites in South India.

famous_mountains.name: Papikonda Hills
famous_mountains.funfact: Part of Papikondalu National Park! The Godavari River cuts through these hills creating spectacular gorges. Popular boat rides.

famous_mountains.name: Nallamala Hills
famous_mountains.funfact: Home to the Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam Tiger Reserve - India's largest! Also contains the famous Srisailam Jyotirlinga temple.

famous_mountains.name: Horsley Hills
famous_mountains.funfact: Named after British collector W.D. Horsley who discovered it in 1870! Called 'Andhra Ooty'. Has pleasant year-round climate.

famous_treks.name: Araku Valley Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Walk through coffee plantations and tribal villages! The Borra Caves here have million-year-old stalactites and stalagmites. The valley is home to over 19 tribes with unique cultures.

famous_treks.name: Mahendra Giri Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Sacred to Lord Parashurama who is believed to have meditated here! Has temples dating back to the 8th century. One of the least-visited peaks in Eastern Ghats.

famous_treks.name: Deomali Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Trek to the highest point in Odisha! The area has unusual pine forests (rare in Eastern Ghats). The region is called 'Kashmir of Odisha' for its pleasant climate.

famous_treks.name: Kolli Hills Trek
famous_treks.funfact: The road has 70 continuous hairpin bends - one of the most winding roads in India! The 300-foot Agaya Gangai waterfall is reached by climbing down 1,300 steps. Ancient tribal traditions still survive here.

famous_treks.name: Yercaud (Shevaroy Hills) Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Called 'Jewel of the South' and 'Poor Man's Ooty'! The Servarayan Temple opens only during the Shevaroy festival once a year. Views extend to the Salem plains below.

famous_treks.name: Javadi Hills Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of the least explored hill ranges in South India! The Malayali tribes here have preserved ancient traditions. Very few tourists venture here.

famous_treks.name: Papikondalu Gorge Trek/Boat
famous_treks.funfact: The Godavari River cuts through the mountains creating spectacular gorges! Traditional paper boats navigate the rapids. The Bison Hills offer treks into the gorge.

famous_treks.name: Nallamala Forest Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Trek through India's largest tiger reserve! The forests hide the ancient Srisailam temple, one of the 12 Jyotirlingas. The Krishna River cuts a dramatic gorge through the hills.

famous_treks.name: Horsley Hills Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Named after British collector who 'discovered' it in 1870! Has India's oldest tree house. The climate is pleasant year-round due to the elevation.

famous_treks.name: Pachamalai Hills Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Home to the Malayali tribe who worship sacred groves! The hills are covered in jackfruit trees - a major livelihood source. Remote and rarely visited by tourists.

eastern_ghats.funfact: Unlike the continuous Western Ghats, the Eastern Ghats are broken by every major east-flowing river! This is why they're not a true mountain range but a series of disconnected hills. They're also much older - some rocks here are 1.6 billion years old!

nilgiri_hills.name: Nilgiri Hills
nilgiri_hills.meaning: Nila (Blue) + Giri (Mountain) - Named for the blue Neelakurinji flowers
nilgiri_hills.location: Tamil Nadu (primarily), with extensions into Kerala and Karnataka
nilgiri_hills.part_of: Junction of Western and Eastern Ghats
nilgiri_hills.unesco_status: Part of Western Ghats World Heritage Site

famous_mountains.name: Doddabetta
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in the Nilgiris and Tamil Nadu! Has a telescope house at the summit. On clear days, you can see as far as Mysore. The peak is often shrouded in mist.

famous_mountains.name: Mukurthi Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Part of Mukurthi National Park, home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr. The peak is sacred to the Toda tribe. Permits required for trekking.

famous_mountains.name: Kundah Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Part of the Kundah Range that feeds the famous Pykara waterfalls. The area has multiple small hydroelectric projects.

famous_mountains.name: Nilgiri Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: The peak that gave the entire range its name! Appears blue due to the Neelakurinji flowers that bloom every 12 years.

famous_mountains.name: Kolaribetta
famous_mountains.funfact: Third highest peak in the Nilgiris. Less visited than Doddabetta. Offers stunning views of the Western Ghats.

famous_treks.name: Doddabetta Peak Trek
famous_treks.funfact: A road goes almost to the top, but the trek through shola forests is more rewarding! On clear days, the views extend to Mysore and beyond. The peak is the highest in Tamil Nadu.

famous_treks.name: Mukurthi Peak Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of the best treks to see the endangered Nilgiri Tahr in the wild! The Mukurthi Lake is sacred to the Toda tribe. Very limited permits issued to protect the ecosystem.

famous_treks.name: Avalanche Lake Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Named after a massive landslide in 1823 that created the lake! The waters are icy cold. Trout fishing is permitted. Indian Gaur (bison) are commonly spotted.

famous_treks.name: Pykara Trek
famous_treks.funfact: The Pykara River drops in two beautiful cascades! Toda tribal settlements are nearby. The pine forests here were planted by the British.

famous_treks.name: Kotagiri to Kodanad Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Kotagiri is the oldest hill station in the Nilgiris! The views from Kodanad viewpoint are spectacular. Catherine Falls is one of the highest in Tamil Nadu.

famous_treks.name: Ooty to Pykara Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Walk through eucalyptus and pine forests planted by the British! Pass traditional Toda hamlets. The Todas have a unique barrel-shaped hut design found nowhere else.

famous_treks.name: Parsons Valley Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Lesser-known gem near Ooty! The reservoir is surrounded by meadows and shola forests. Excellent for bird watching and landscape photography.

toda_tribe.name: Toda Tribe
toda_tribe.funfact: The Todas have lived in the Nilgiris for centuries and have their own unique culture including buffalo worship. Their barrel-shaped huts (dogles) are found nowhere else on Earth. Their embroidery is a UNESCO-recognized craft.

neelakurinji_bloom.funfact: The Nilgiris get their name from this flower! When it blooms, entire mountainsides turn blue-purple. The Paliyan tribe used to calculate their age by counting the blooms.

nilgiri_hills.funfact: The Nilgiris are at the junction of the Western and Eastern Ghats - a unique ecological convergence! This makes them extremely biodiverse. The British established Ooty here as a summer capital to escape the heat of the plains.

palani_hills.name: Palani Hills

famous_mountains.name: Perumal Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in the Palani Hills! Sacred to local tribes. Offers panoramic views of the entire range.

famous_mountains.name: Vandaravu Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Actually the highest point near Kodaikanal! Less visited than other peaks. Offers stunning views of the Vaigai Dam.

famous_mountains.name: Palani Hills (Main)
famous_mountains.funfact: Site of the famous Murugan Temple at Palani - one of the six abodes of Lord Murugan. Pilgrims climb 699 steps to reach the temple.

famous_treks.name: Dolphin's Nose Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Named for the rock that resembles a dolphin's nose! Offers views of the Palani Plains 2,000 meters below. On clear days, you can see Madurai.

famous_treks.name: Perumal Peak Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Trek to the highest peak in the Palani Hills! The route passes through pristine shola forests. Very few tourists attempt this trek.

famous_treks.name: Palani Hills Temple Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Climb 699 steps to one of the most important Murugan temples! Alternatively, use the winch or rope car. Millions of pilgrims visit annually.

famous_treks.name: Pambar Shola Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Walk through one of the last undisturbed shola forests! Home to several endemic bird species. The forest floor is carpeted with ferns and mosses.

famous_treks.name: Berijam Lake Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of the most beautiful lakes in South India! Access is restricted to protect the ecosystem. The lake supplies water to Kodaikanal.

palani_hills.funfact: Kodaikanal was established by American missionaries in 1845, not the British! It's called the 'Princess of Hill Stations'. The Palani Temple here is one of six sacred abodes of Lord Murugan.

anamalai_hills.name: Anamalai Hills

famous_mountains.name: Anamudi
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in South India! The shape resembles an elephant's forehead. Home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr. Permits required for climbing.

famous_mountains.name: Meesapulimala
famous_mountains.funfact: Second highest peak in South India! The mountain profile looks like a moustached leopard. One of the most challenging treks in the region.

famous_mountains.name: Kolukkumalai
famous_mountains.funfact: Home to the world's highest tea plantation! The sunrise views are legendary. Accessible by jeep on steep dirt roads.

famous_treks.name: Anamudi Summit Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Summit the highest peak south of the Himalayas! The trek passes through prime Nilgiri Tahr habitat. Limited permits ensure minimal human impact.

famous_treks.name: Meesapulimala Trek
famous_treks.funfact: The mountain looks like a moustached leopard! One of the most rewarding treks in South India. The night camping on the grasslands is magical.

famous_treks.name: Kolukkumalai Sunrise Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Watch the sunrise from the world's highest tea plantation! The jeep ride up is an adventure itself. The century-old tea factory still uses orthodox methods.

famous_treks.name: Chinnar Wildlife Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Unique dry ecosystem in otherwise wet Western Ghats! Home to the endangered grizzled giant squirrel. Muthuvan tribal guides lead the treks.

famous_treks.name: Top Station Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Called 'Top Station' because it was the top point of a ropeway! On clear days, you can see the plains of Tamil Nadu 2,000 meters below. Neelakurinji blooms here every 12 years.

anamalai_hills.funfact: The Anamalai Hills contain the highest peak in South India and are named after the elephants that roam here! The region has the highest density of Asian elephants in India. The Anamalai Tiger Reserve is one of the best-protected forests in India.

cardamom_hills.name: Cardamom Hills
cardamom_hills.meaning: Named after cardamom spice grown here

famous_mountains.name: Anaerangal
famous_mountains.funfact: Features a scenic lake surrounded by tea estates. Popular photography destination.

famous_mountains.name: Mattupetty
famous_mountains.funfact: Has a famous reservoir surrounded by tea gardens and forests. Echo point nearby.

famous_treks.name: Munnar Tea Plantation Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Walk through endless carpets of tea! Munnar produces some of the world's finest tea. The tea museum showcases the industry's colonial history.

famous_treks.name: Periyar Tiger Reserve Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of the few tiger reserves where you can trek inside! Options range from nature walks to full border hikes. Former poachers now work as guides.

famous_treks.name: Vagamon Meadows Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Called 'Mini Scotland' for its rolling green meadows! The pine forests were planted by the British. Kurisumala Ashram offers spiritual retreats.

famous_treks.name: Gavi Trek
famous_treks.funfact: One of Kerala's best-kept secrets! Limited entries protect this pristine ecosystem. Excellent chances of seeing Indian Gaur (bison) and elephants.

cardamom_hills.funfact: The Cardamom Hills produce 60% of India's cardamom - the 'Queen of Spices'! The spice has been traded from here for over 3,000 years. Ancient Romans paid exorbitant prices for cardamom from these hills.

purvanchal_range.name: Purvanchal Range
purvanchal_range.location: Northeast India (Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, Meghalaya)

sub_ranges.name: Patkai Range
sub_ranges.funfact: Forms the India-Myanmar border. The word 'Patkai' means 'chicken cut' in local language referring to a legendary event.

sub_ranges.name: Naga Hills
sub_ranges.funfact: Home to the Naga tribes with rich warrior traditions. The famous Hornbill Festival celebrates Naga culture.

sub_ranges.name: Manipur Hills
sub_ranges.funfact: Surround the Manipur Valley creating a bowl-shaped landscape. Home to the unique Loktak Lake with floating islands.

sub_ranges.name: Mizo Hills (Lushai Hills)
sub_ranges.funfact: The most southerly extension of the Himalayan system. The blue mountain (Phawngpui) is sacred to the Mizo people.

sub_ranges.name: Garo-Khasi-Jaintia Hills
sub_ranges.funfact: Meghalaya means 'Abode of Clouds'. Cherrapunji and Mawsynram here receive the highest rainfall on Earth!

sub_ranges.name: Barail Range
sub_ranges.funfact: Separates the Brahmaputra and Barak river basins. Forms the boundary between Naga and Manipuri cultures.

sub_ranges.name: Mishmi Hills
sub_ranges.funfact: Among the least explored mountains in India! Home to the Mishmi tribe and the endangered Mishmi Takin.

famous_mountains.name: Saramati
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in Nagaland! On the India-Myanmar border. Rhododendrons bloom spectacularly here. Snow-capped in winter - unusual for this latitude.

famous_mountains.name: Phawngpui (Blue Mountain)
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest peak in Mizoram! Appears blue from a distance due to the flowers. Sacred to the Mizo people. Has unique orchids found nowhere else.

famous_mountains.name: Mount Iso (Shirui Peak)
famous_mountains.funfact: Home to the rare Shirui Lily found nowhere else on Earth! The lily blooms in May-June and is celebrated with a festival.

famous_mountains.name: Japvo Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Considered sacred by the Angami Naga tribe. The summit offers views of both Manipur and Nagaland. Dense rhododendron forests cover the slopes.

famous_mountains.name: Shillong Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest point in Meghalaya! Offers views of the entire Shillong Plateau. An Air Force radar station sits at the top. Named 'U-Shulong' by Khasis.

famous_mountains.name: Mount Harriet
famous_mountains.location: Andaman Islands (different region but related geologically)
famous_mountains.funfact: Highest point in the Andaman Islands! Named after Harriet Tytler. Has a remnant of tropical rainforest.

famous_mountains.name: Nokrek Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Part of Nokrek National Park - home to wild citrus varieties, ancestors of all modern oranges! UNESCO Biosphere Reserve.

famous_mountains.name: Mawphlang Sacred Peak
famous_mountains.funfact: Site of the famous Mawphlang Sacred Grove - an ancient forest protected for centuries by tribal law. Nothing can be removed from the grove.

famous_treks.name: Dzukou Valley Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Called the 'Valley of Flowers of the Northeast'! Home to the rare Dzukou Lily found nowhere else. The undulating hills look like green waves.

famous_treks.name: Saramati Peak Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Trek through Naga tribal lands to Nagaland's highest peak! Snow caps the summit in winter. The rhododendrons in bloom are spectacular.

famous_treks.name: Phawngpui (Blue Mountain) Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Trek to Mizoram's highest and most sacred peak! Appears blue from distance due to rare flowers. The mountain is protected as a national park.

famous_treks.name: Shirui Peak (Mount Iso) Trek
famous_treks.funfact: The ONLY place on Earth where the Shirui Lily grows! The flower blooms in May-June. The Shirui Lily Festival celebrates this natural wonder.

famous_treks.name: Living Root Bridges Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Walk on bridges made from living tree roots! The Khasi people train rubber tree roots to grow into bridges over 30 meters long. Some are 500+ years old and still growing!

famous_treks.name: David Scott Trail
famous_treks.funfact: Named after a British administrator who built this trail in the 1800s to link Bangladesh (then Bengal) to Assam. Passes through beautiful Khasi countryside.

famous_treks.name: Nongriat Double Decker Bridge Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Descend 3,500 steps to see the world-famous double-decker living root bridge! The only double-decker in the world. The swimming pool at Rainbow Falls is crystal clear.

famous_treks.name: Nohkalikai Falls Trek
famous_treks.funfact: India's tallest plunge waterfall at 340 meters! Named after a woman who jumped to her death after a tragedy. The blue pool at the base is mesmerizing.

famous_treks.name: Mawphlang Sacred Grove Trek
famous_treks.funfact: Walk through a forest protected for centuries by tribal law! Nothing can be removed - even a fallen leaf. The forest is a living pharmacy with hundreds of medicinal plants.

famous_treks.name: Keibul Lamjao Trek/Canoe
famous_treks.funfact: The only floating national park in the world! Home to the endangered Sangai (dancing deer). The park floats on phumdis (floating vegetation) on Loktak Lake.

purvanchal_range.funfact: The Purvanchal mountains are the southward continuation of the Himalayas! They curve around the head of the Brahmaputra River. The region receives some of the highest rainfall on Earth - Mawsynram (11,871mm) and Cherrapunji are here.

shillong_plateau.name: Shillong Plateau

famous_features.name: Cherrapunji (Sohra)
famous_features.funfact: Once held the record for highest rainfall! Features stunning waterfalls and living root bridges.

famous_features.name: Mawsynram
famous_features.funfact: Current holder of the record for highest average annual rainfall on Earth! Even wetter than Cherrapunji.

shillong_plateau.funfact: The Shillong Plateau is geologically separate from the Himalayas - it's a piece of the ancient Indian peninsula that uplifted! It's made of rocks 600 million years old, while the Himalayas are only 50 million years old.

interesting_facts.fact: The Himalayas are still growing at about 1cm per year!
interesting_facts.funfact: The collision between the Indian and Eurasian plates continues. This is why earthquakes are common in the Himalayan region.

interesting_facts.fact: The Aravallis are older than the Himalayas by 1.75 billion years!
interesting_facts.funfact: The Aravallis were once as tall as the Himalayas but have eroded over billions of years. They're among the oldest mountains on Earth.

interesting_facts.fact: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas
interesting_facts.funfact: The Western Ghats formed 150 million years ago when India separated from Africa. They're a remnant of the ancient supercontinent Gondwana.

interesting_facts.fact: India has peaks over 8,000m, 7,000m, 6,000m, and numerous peaks above 5,000m
interesting_facts.funfact: Only Nepal has more 8,000m peaks. India has the third highest peak in the world (Kangchenjunga).

interesting_facts.fact: The Chadar Trek is disappearing due to climate change
interesting_facts.funfact: The Zanskar River doesn't freeze as solidly as it used to. Scientists predict the trek may become impossible within decades.

interesting_facts.fact: Mount Kailash has never been climbed
interesting_facts.funfact: Despite being only 6,638m (climbable height), Mount Kailash remains unclimbed out of respect for its sacred status to Hindus, Buddhists, Jains, and Bon followers.

interesting_facts.fact: Roopkund Lake contains hundreds of ancient human skeletons
interesting_facts.funfact: Scientists believe these were 9th-century pilgrims killed by a sudden hailstorm. DNA analysis shows they were from different populations across Asia and the Mediterranean!

interesting_facts.fact: Living root bridges in Meghalaya are grown, not built
interesting_facts.funfact: Khasi tribes train rubber tree roots to grow across streams. These living bridges strengthen with age and can last 500+ years. Some are over 30 meters long.

interesting_facts.fact: The Neelakurinji flower blooms only once every 12 years
interesting_facts.funfact: When it blooms, entire mountainsides in the Nilgiris turn blue-purple. The Paliyan tribe used to calculate their age by counting the blooms.

interesting_facts.fact: Mawsynram receives more rainfall than almost any other place on Earth
interesting_facts.funfact: It receives 11,871mm of rainfall annually - that's nearly 40 feet of rain! Ironically, the region faces water shortages in dry season because the rain runs off quickly.

interesting_facts.fact: The Valley of Flowers was unknown to the outside world until 1931
interesting_facts.funfact: British mountaineer Frank Smythe 'discovered' it after losing his way. Over 600 species of flowering plants bloom here, many found nowhere else.

interesting_facts.fact: Sandakphu is the only place to see 4 of the world's 5 highest peaks
interesting_facts.funfact: From this ridge on the India-Nepal border, you can see Everest, Kangchenjunga, Lhotse, and Makalu simultaneously. No other point on Earth offers this view.

interesting_facts.fact: The Shirui Lily grows only on one mountain peak in the world
interesting_facts.funfact: Found only on Mount Iso (Shirui Peak) in Manipur. Attempts to grow it elsewhere have failed. The Shirui Lily Festival celebrates this natural wonder.

interesting_facts.fact: Stok Kangri is one of the easiest 6,000m peaks to climb
interesting_facts.funfact: No technical climbing skills are needed. Thousands of trekkers summit it each year, making it a popular 'bucket list' peak for adventure seekers.

interesting_facts.fact: The Aravallis stop the Thar Desert from spreading
interesting_facts.funfact: Without the Aravallis, the Thar Desert would expand eastward into the fertile Gangetic plains. These ancient mountains serve as a crucial ecological barrier.
